word: The new policy brought about sweeping reforms.
explanation: <Brought about> means to cause something significant to happen

word: The scandal set off a chain reaction in parliament.
explanation: <Set off> means to trigger or initiate a series of events

word: Years of neglect gave rise to widespread discontent.
explanation: <Gave rise to> means to cause something to develop or emerge

word: The merger brought forth unprecedented challenges.
explanation: <Brought forth> means to produce or generate something new

word: The crisis touched off a wave of protests.
explanation: <Touched off> means to cause something sudden and dramatic to begin

word: The professor expounded on the theory at length.
explanation: <Expounded on> means to explain or present in great detail

word: She touched upon the ethical implications briefly.
explanation: <Touched upon> means to mention or address a topic briefly

word: The critic tore apart the author's central thesis.
explanation: <Tore apart> means to criticize severely and systematically

word: He honed in on the weakest point of the argument.
explanation: <Honed in on> means to focus precisely on a specific aspect

word: The panel thrashed out a compromise after hours of debate.
explanation: <Thrashed out> means to discuss thoroughly until reaching a resolution

word: The committee drew up a comprehensive framework.
explanation: <Drew up> means to prepare or draft a formal document or plan

word: They mapped out a five-year strategic vision.
explanation: <Mapped out> means to plan something in careful detail

word: The board pencilled in a provisional timeline.
explanation: <Pencilled in> means to schedule tentatively, subject to change

word: We need to factor in the geopolitical implications.
explanation: <Factor in> means to include something as a relevant consideration

word: The government phased out subsidies over three years.
explanation: <Phased out> means to discontinue something gradually and systematically

word: She saw through his elaborate pretence immediately.
explanation: <Saw through> means to recognize deception or insincerity

word: The auditor picked up on subtle discrepancies in the accounts.
explanation: <Picked up on> means to notice something that others might miss

word: He read into her silence more than was warranted.
explanation: <Read into> means to interpret meaning beyond what is explicitly stated

word: The jury weighed up the conflicting testimonies carefully.
explanation: <Weighed up> means to assess or evaluate something thoroughly

word: She cottoned on to the underlying agenda rather quickly.
explanation: <Cottoned on to> means to begin to understand something not immediately obvious

word: The diplomat smoothed over the tensions between delegations.
explanation: <Smoothed over> means to make a problem seem less serious or resolve friction

word: He talked her round to accepting the proposal.
explanation: <Talked round> means to persuade someone to change their mind

word: The mediator prevailed upon both parties to compromise.
explanation: <Prevailed upon> means to persuade someone through sustained effort

word: She fobbed off the journalist with a vague statement.
explanation: <Fobbed off> means to dismiss or deceive someone with something inadequate

word: The manager palmed off the tedious task onto an intern.
explanation: <Palmed off> means to pass something unwanted to someone else through deception

word: The investigation unearthed systemic corruption.
explanation: <Unearthed> means to discover something hidden or previously unknown

word: The team ferreted out the source of the data breach.
explanation: <Ferreted out> means to search tenaciously until finding something concealed

word: They ironed out the remaining contractual disputes.
explanation: <Ironed out> means to resolve problems or difficulties

word: The researchers stumbled upon a groundbreaking finding.
explanation: <Stumbled upon> means to discover something unexpectedly or by chance

word: The analyst teased out the implications of the data.
explanation: <Teased out> means to extract or identify something with careful effort
